transport.c(3) Library Functions Manual transport.c(3)

NAME

transport.c - WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64
Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). #define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512
Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). #define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING
#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Functions


acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection.

Detailed Description

WebSocket transport implementation for ACIP protocol.

Implements the acip_transport_t interface for WebSocket connections. Enables browser clients to connect via WebSocket protocol.

ARCHITECTURE:

  • Uses libwebsockets for WebSocket protocol handling
  • Async libwebsockets callbacks bridge to sync recv() via ringbuffer
  • Thread-safe receive queue handles async message arrival
  • Same pattern as WebRTC transport for consistency

MESSAGE FLOW:

1.
send(): Synchronous write via lws_write()
2.
LWS callback: Async write to receive ringbuffer
3.
recv(): Blocking read from receive ringbuffer

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport OWNS wsi (WebSocket instance)
  • Receive queue owns buffered message data
  • recv() allocates message buffer, caller must free

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file websocket/transport.c.

Macro Definition Documentation

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING 64

Maximum incoming message queue size (frames received from peer). Set to 64 for balance: allows buffering across one large frame's fragments while keeping latency reasonable. With ~70 fragments per 291KB frame, we need at least 64 slots to avoid dropping the frame while it's fragmenting.

Definition at line 59 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING 512

Maximum outgoing message queue size (frames to send to peer). Set to 64 to match incoming queue and prevent "BINARY after FIN" errors. Large video frames fragment into ~70 chunks; queue must be large enough to buffer one complete message worth of fragments.

Definition at line 68 of file websocket/transport.c.

#define WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_INCOMING

Definition at line 71 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

#define WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE WEBSOCKET_MESSAGE_QUEUE_SIZE_OUTGOING

Definition at line 72 of file websocket/transport.c.

Referenced by acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), and acip_websocket_server_transport_create().

Function Documentation

acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)

Create WebSocket client transport.

Parameters

url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225")
crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)

Returns

Transport instance or NULL on failure

Definition at line 1379 of file websocket/transport.c.

1380                                                                                        {
1381   if (!name) {
1382     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1383     return NULL;
1384   }
1385 
1386   if (!url) {
1387     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1388     return NULL;
1389   }
1390 
1391   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1392   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1393   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1394   if (!protocol_end) {
1395     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1396     return NULL;
1397   }
1398 
1399   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1400   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1401 
1402   // Find port (if specified)
1403   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1404   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1405 
1406   char host[256] = {0};
1407   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1408   char path[256] = "/";
1409 
1410   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1411     // Port is specified
1412     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1413     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1414       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1415       return NULL;
1416     }
1417     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1418     host[host_len] = '\0';
1419 
1420     // Extract port
1421     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1422     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1423       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1424       return NULL;
1425     }
1426   } else {
1427     // No port specified, use default
1428     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1429     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1430       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1431       return NULL;
1432     }
1433     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1434     host[host_len] = '\0';
1435   }
1436 
1437   // Extract path
1438   if (path_start) {
1439     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1440     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1441   }
1442 
1443   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1444 
1445   // Allocate transport structure
1446   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1447   if (!transport) {
1448     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1449     return NULL;
1450   }
1451 
1452   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1453   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1454       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1455   if (!ws_data) {
1456     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1457     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1458     return NULL;
1459   }
1460 
1461   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1462   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1463 
1464   // Create receive queue
1465   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1466   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1467     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1468     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1469     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1470     return NULL;
1471   }
1472 
1473   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1474   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1475   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1476     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1477     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1478     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1479     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1480     return NULL;
1481   }
1482 
1483   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1484   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1485   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1486   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1487 
1488   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1489     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1490     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1491     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1492     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1493     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1494     return NULL;
1495   }
1496 
1497   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1498     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1499     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1500     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1501     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1502     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1503     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1504     return NULL;
1505   }
1506 
1507   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1508     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1509     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1510     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1511     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1512     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1513     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1514     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1515     return NULL;
1516   }
1517 
1518   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1519     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1520     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1521     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1522     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1523     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1524     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1525     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1526     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1527     return NULL;
1528   }
1529 
1530   // Allocate initial send buffer
1531   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1532   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1533   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1534     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1535     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1536     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1537     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1538     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1539     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1540     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1541     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1542     return NULL;
1543   }
1544 
1545   // Create libwebsockets context
1546   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1547   // NOTE: Define ONLY "acip" protocol for WebSocket connections
1548   // libwebsockets provides built-in "h1" and "http" protocols for WebSocket upgrade handshake.
1549   // When local_protocol_name="h1" and protocol="acip" are specified:
1550   // 1. libwebsockets uses built-in h1 (HTTP/1.1) for initial connection
1551   // 2. HTTP upgrade happens automatically (101 Switching Protocols)
1552   // 3. Connection switches to "acip" protocol using our custom callback
1553   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1554       {
1555           "acip", // ACIP protocol - used after WebSocket upgrade handshake completes
1556           websocket_callback,
1557           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1558           524288, // RX buffer size
1559           0,      // ID
1560           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1561           524288  // TX packet size
1562       },
1563       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1564   };
1565 
1566   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1567   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1568   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1569   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1570   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1571   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1572   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1573 
1574   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1575   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1576   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1577   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1578   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1579   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1580   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1581   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1582   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1583   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1584 
1585   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1586   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1587   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1588   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1589 
1590   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1591   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1592   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1593       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1594       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1595   };
1596   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1597 
1598   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1599   lws_log_init_client();
1600 
1601   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1602 
1603   if (!ws_data->context) {
1604     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1605     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1606     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1607     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1608     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1609     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1610     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1611     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1612     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1613     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1614     return NULL;
1615   }
1616 
1617   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1618   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1619   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1620   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1621   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1622 
1623   // Connect to WebSocket server
1624   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1625   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1626   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1627   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1628   connect_info.address = host;
1629   connect_info.port = port;
1630   connect_info.path = path;
1631   connect_info.host = host;
1632   connect_info.origin = host;
1633   // Use h1 (HTTP/1.1) protocol for WebSocket upgrade with acip as the subprotocol
1634   // - local_protocol_name="h1": libwebsockets' built-in HTTP/1.1 handler for WebSocket upgrade
1635   // - protocol="acip": Request this subprotocol from the remote server (Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header)
1636   // libwebsockets internally uses h1 regardless of the protocol name, so we use h1 directly
1637   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "h1";  // libwebsockets built-in HTTP/1.1
1638   connect_info.protocol = "acip";  // Remote subprotocol to request from server
1639   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1640   connect_info.ssl_connection = use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1641   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1642 
1643   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1644   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1645   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1646   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1647     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1648     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1649     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1650     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1651     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1652     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1653     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1654     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1655     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1656     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1657     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1658     return NULL;
1659   }
1660 
1661   // Initialize transport
1662   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1663   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1664   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1665 
1666   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1667   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1668   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1669   ws_data->service_running = true;
1670   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1671     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1672     ws_data->service_running = false;
1673     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1674     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1675     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1676     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1677     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1678     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1679     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1680     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1681     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1682     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1683     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1684     return NULL;
1685   }
1686   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1687 
1688   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1689   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1690   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1691   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1692   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1693   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1694 
1695   // Register transport
1696   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1697 
1698   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1699   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1700 
1701   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1702   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1703   return transport;
1704 }




References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), lws_log_init_client(),
    mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(), ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1716 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1717                                                                                        {
1718   if (!name) {
1719     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1720     return NULL;
1721   }
1722 
1723   if (!wsi) {
1724     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1725     return NULL;
1726   }
1727 
1728   // Allocate transport structure
1729   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1730   if (!transport) {
1731     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1732     return NULL;
1733   }
1734 
1735   // Allocate transport-specific data
1736   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1737       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1738   if (!ws_data) {
1739     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1740     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1741     return NULL;
1742   }
1743 
1744   // Initialize receive queue
1745   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1746   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1747     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1748     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1749     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1750     return NULL;
1751   }
1752 
1753   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1754   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1755   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1756     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1757     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1758     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1759     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1760     return NULL;
1761   }
1762 
1763   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1764   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1765   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1766   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1767   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1768     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1769     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1770     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1771     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1772     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1773     return NULL;
1774   }
1775 
1776   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1777   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1778   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1779   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1780   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1781   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1782 
1783   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1784     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1785     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1786     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1787     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1788     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1789     return NULL;
1790   }
1791 
1792   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1793     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1794     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1795     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1796     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1797     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1798     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1799     return NULL;
1800   }
1801 
1802   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1803     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1804     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1805     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1806     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1807     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1808     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1809     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1810     return NULL;
1811   }
1812 
1813   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1814     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1815     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1816     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1817     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1818     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1819     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1820     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1821     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1822     return NULL;
1823   }
1824 
1825   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1826     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1827     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1828     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1829     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1830     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1831     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1832     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1833     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1834     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1835     return NULL;
1836   }
1837 
1838   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1839     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1840     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1841     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1842     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1843     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1844     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1845     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1846     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1847     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1848     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1849     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1850     return NULL;
1851   }
1852 
1853   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1854   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1855   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1856   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1857     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1858     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1859     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1860     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1861     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1862     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1863     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1864     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1865     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1866     return NULL;
1867   }
1868   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1869 
1870   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1871   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1872   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1873   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1874   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1875   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1876            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1877   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1878 
1879   // Initialize transport
1880   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1881   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1882   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1883 
1884   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1885 
1886   // Register transport
1887   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1888 
1889   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1890   NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1891 
1892   return transport;
1893 }






References mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    ringbuffer_create(), ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SET_ERRNO, WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
